implemented: Rewrote Language::from_directory in crates/tldr-core/src/types.rs to apply manifest priority BEFORE extension majority. New algorithm: (1) Scan root, each immediate subdir, and each grandchild (depth <= 2 — covers apps/*/ and packages/*/ monorepo layouts) for a build manifest, skipping hidden dirs and the walker's DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS (node_modules, target, dist, build, .next, __pycache__, vendor, .git). Among all manifests found, the one with the highest precedence wins; ties at same precedence break on shallowest depth. Precedence order: tsconfig.json > package.json > Cargo.toml > go.mod > pyproject.toml/setup.py/requirements.txt > pom.xml > build.gradle.kts/build.gradle > Gemfile > composer.json > mix.exs > Package.swift. package.json returns TypeScript when a `"typescript": ...` dep is declared (cheap substring+colon heuristic, no JSON parser dep), else JavaScript. build.gradle.kts tie-breaks Kotlin vs Java by counting .kt/.kts vs .java across the walk. (2) If no manifest found, fall back to extension majority via walk_project (which already skips node_modules, honors .gitignore, does not follow symlinks). Deleted duplicates: crates/tldr-core/src/surface/mod.rs lost fn detect_lang_from_directory and fn detect_lang_from_directory_recursive (58 lines). crates/tldr-core/src/quality/{dead_code,health,martin}.rs each lost fn detect_language (~28 lines each). Each deletion site now inlines `if path.is_file() { Language::from_path(path).ok_or_else(...) } else { Language::from_directory(path).ok_or_else(...) }`. surface::detect_lang_from_path now delegates to Language::from_directory via as_str(). Rationale for precedence-beats-depth: in pnpm monorepos the root package.json holds tooling only (turbo, prettier, eslint) while real TS lives in packages/ui/tsconfig.json — a depth-first rule would return JavaScript (wrong), tsconfig-beats-package-json returns TypeScript (correct, matches dub acceptance).

corrections: Initial design scanned only depth 0 (root) + depth 1 (immediate subdirs) per literal reading of task spec — but the spec also said 'monorepos often have manifests in packages/*/' which is depth 2. Depth-1-only would have returned JavaScript for dub (root package.json has no typescript dep, apps/ and packages/ at depth 1 have no manifests, tsconfig.json lives at depth 2 in apps/web/ and packages/ui/). Extended scanning depth to <= 2 to cover this case.

Initial design: shallowest-path-wins + precedence-for-ties. This made a root package.json (no TS dep) return JavaScript even when nested tsconfig.json files exist. Changed to: precedence-wins + shallowest-for-ties. Now tsconfig.json at depth 2 beats package.json at depth 0 → TypeScript wins for dub. Added test_from_directory_pnpm_monorepo_with_bare_root_package_json to pin this behavior.

clippy flagged a `for m in present { match { return Some(...) } ... }` loop as never-looping (all arms return unconditionally). Refactored to `let m = *present.first()?; let lang = match m { ... }; Some(lang)` — cleaner and matches actual semantics (the first element is always the winner since present is pre-sorted by precedence).

discoveries.finding: dub root package.json has NO `typescript` dep — only turbo/prettier/eslint tooling. Real TypeScript lives in apps/web/tsconfig.json and packages/ui/tsconfig.json (both depth 2). A depth-1-only manifest scan would have returned JavaScript — the fix was to extend to depth 2 AND use precedence-beats-depth so tsconfig.json anywhere beats package.json anywhere.

cargo fmt -- <path> does NOT format only those paths; on this workspace it formatted 90+ files (all with pre-existing fmt drift). Staged only the 5 files I actually edited so the commit stays focused; the pre-existing workspace fmt drift is outside VAL-002 scope (m1 report already flagged this).

ProjectWalker was imported but unused in quality/dead_code.rs and quality/martin.rs after detect_language deletion (only walk_project still used). Dropped the ProjectWalker from both imports to keep clippy clean without a suppress.

HashMap was imported in quality/health.rs solely for the deleted detect_language's local count map. Other functions in health.rs use IndexMap, not HashMap. Dropped the `use std::collections::HashMap;` line to silence the unused_imports warning without suppressing it.

conventions: Directory-level language detection has exactly ONE entry point: Language::from_directory at crates/tldr-core/src/types.rs. Do NOT add `fn detect_lang_from_directory` or a local `fn detect_language(path: &Path) -> TldrResult<Language>` in any new command — inline `Language::from_path(path).ok_or_else(...)` for files and `Language::from_directory(path).ok_or_else(...)` for directories.

Manifest priority in from_directory is strict precedence: lower precedence index wins regardless of depth. Add new manifest kinds to MANIFEST_PRECEDENCE in the correct slot (higher-confidence manifests earlier) and extend language_from_manifest_set with the new arm.

package.json is probed via a cheap substring heuristic (check for `"typescript"` key with colon) to avoid pulling serde_json into the hot detection path. If a future manifest rule needs richer JSON reads, factor out and share a single read — don't add another ad-hoc parser.
